archetype: Closed-Loop Electroporation Pulse Waveform Synthesizer & Bio-Impedance Feedback Controller for Ex Vivo Organ Perfusion
description: A hard real-time control system that orchestrates microsecond-precision high-voltage electroporation pulses on donor organs during ex vivo perfusion to facilitate gene therapy delivery or cryoprotectant introduction. It actively monitors multi-frequency bio-impedance spectroscopy data to dynamically adjust pulse amplitude and duration to prevent cellular lysis while ensuring transfection efficiency. The system operates in a sterile, isolated environment requiring sub-millisecond latency to prevent irreversible tissue damage.
rules: MUST adhere to IEC 60601-2-25 requirements for electro-medical diagnostic equipment safety and leakage current limits.
MUST utilize IEEE 11073 SDC (Service Oriented Device Connectivity) for real-time communication with the organ perfusion console.
MUST process bio-impedance spectra across 10kHz to 10MHz bandwidth with a sampling jitter not exceeding 50 nanoseconds.
MUST implement deterministic scheduling (e.g., RT-PREEMPT kernel) to guarantee pulse generation within 200 microseconds of feedback trigger.
MUST log all waveform parameters and impedance shifts to a WORM (Write Once Read Many) storage compliant with 21 CFR Part 11 for clinical audit trails.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard TCP/IP networking for the feedback loop, as packet retransmission latency variations can cause lethal over-exposure to electrical fields.
DO NOT rely on non-deterministic garbage-collected languages for the pulse generation thread, as pause times will corrupt the transfection protocol.
DO NOT implement 'best-effort' error handling; any deviation in voltage must trigger an immediate hardware-level fail-safe shutdown rather than a software retry.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing hardware-triggered high-voltage discharge with high-frequency analog-to-digital conversion without clock drift or jitter.
Implementing adaptive control algorithms that model the non-linear, time-varying electrical properties of biological tissue in real-time.
Designing isolation barriers and shielding to prevent electromagnetic interference (EMI) from corrupting sensitive bio-sensor readings during high-voltage events.

recommended_stack.language: Rust + C FFI — Provides memory safety guarantees for the main logic while enabling direct, zero-copy access to FPGA registers for pulse timing.
recommended_stack.framework: FreeRTOS or QNX Neutrino — Offers hard real-time deterministic scheduling required for microsecond-level control loops.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB with Hypertables — Optimized for time-series ingestion of high-frequency impedance telemetry and audit logging.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (ZMQ) — Provides low-latency, deterministic pub/sub messaging for inter-process communication within the local control node.
